artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

I’m finally sitting down with the laptop at the kitchen table — Becca’s already gone to bed because she has early patients at the clinic tomorrow — and I wanted to actually write instead of just sending you half‑asleep texts.

Work has been…a lot. Ever since that 11/02/2025 “Difficult Conversations” workshop at the Midwest Health Alliance downtown training room where I froze in front of everyone, I’ve been more careful about what I pile on my plate. I ended up scheduling an emergency telehealth with Dr. Priya Raman at Lakeshore Behavioral the next morning from the office, and she was very blunt that I can’t keep recreating my own panic triggers in my games on top of the LMS stress.

It’s funny, because I’ve been pouring so much of that into Avaris. The Dominion of Virel’s inquisitors are basically our compliance team with swords, and the way Lady Serene is boxed in by League bankers is just me reliving Dad’s bills from Rush. Remember in March 2024 when he had that minor heart attack at Rush University Medical Center and the $4,800 out‑of‑pocket hit? I put $2,000 of that on my Chase Freedom and took three extra Veiled Gate Games gigs that month just so you and Dad wouldn’t have to think about it. Now, in the campaign, Serene is doing the same thing with grain loans from Marisport.

Dr. Raman keeps telling me it’s okay to use the game to process things as long as I’m not drowning in it. So I’m trying to keep the panic and dissociation stuff abstracted — no more first‑person “can’t breathe, time skipping” descriptions like what actually happens to me on the Brown Line.

Anyway, we’re still planning to come out to Oak Park on Sunday. Text me what you want us to bring besides dessert.

Love,
Jordan

Hey Erin,

I owe you like seventeen updates, so here’s a brain dump while my Foundry server is rebooting.

Book 2 of Shadows Over Avaris went feral after Thanksgiving. I realized on the drive back from Naperville that the Baron of Highgate is just Dad with a different hat. That whole blow‑up over Thanksgiving weekend 11/28–11/30/2024 — him refusing to help Aunt Theresa with the $4,800 in credit card debt she ran up covering Lena’s daycare while your aunt was in rehab — basically turned straight into the scene where the Baron shrugs off his sister’s grain debts. I even drafted the confrontation in our living room at 1842 N Hoyne as soon as we got home.

Layered on top of that is the smuggler‑guild debt nonsense I stole from Dad’s own mess. When I was at their place in Naperville after his second minor stroke at Rush in November 2023 and he put those collection calls on speaker, hearing the guy calmly say there was “no realistic path out” of that $18,700 in medical and Visa debt stuck in my head. Now the guild contracts in Book 3 use the same interest math.

And because I apparently don’t know when to stop cannibalizing my life, the disgraced temple inquisitor in Book 3 is basically my July–September 2021 HR saga at Midwest Health Alliance. Elena’s “failure to align with organizational values” line from the DEI microlearning PIP? It’s literally in his trial transcript in my draft. I know I need to file the serial numbers off before I ever show this to anyone outside the table.

On the upside, Lena is obsessed with the lion on my Caldria map and keeps asking if Lady Serene has a kitty. So at least some of this is landing softly somewhere.

Love,
J

Obsidian note – Campaign / Life bleed check‑in
Date: 2024‑03‑22

1. Money + guild debt

I need to be honest that the cult initiation “you can never get out” debt in Book 1 is just my own numbers with different currency. As of January 2024 I still owe about $18,600 in federal + private DePaul loans, and when Nelnet bumped my payment from $210 to $347 on 10/01/2023 after the pandemic forbearance ended, I literally paused two Thursday Roll20 campaigns so I could afford rent here on Hoyne. Then in April 2024 I realized I’d under‑reported ~$3,800 in Veiled Gate income from the Roll20 Chicago group and almost triggered an IRS underpayment penalty. You sat with me at your Logan Square place for four hours sorting receipts and drafting the payment plan. All of that is sitting under the smuggler‑guild mechanics.

2. Health + Veil‑sickness

Veil‑sickness in Book 3 is my October–December 2020 burnout at Rush’s outpatient behavioral clinic with Dr. Priya Narang: 3 a.m. wake‑ups, panic in Zoom meetings, the sertraline bump to 50 mg. I’ve basically copied my CBT homework into the condition tables. That’s fine for my eyes, but if I ever publish this I need to strip the identifiers.

3. Boundaries

No more new plots about corrupt tax assessors or debtors’ prisons until the IRS balance is gone. It’s starting to feel less like catharsis and more like picking at a scab.

Event: Saturday GURPS – Home group (Mana 2 only)
Date: 2024-11-16
Time: 6:30–10:00 p.m.
Location: Living room, 1842 N Hoyne Ave, Apt 3F
Attendees: Jordan, Becca, Erin, Sam, Alex (online)

Notes:

– This is a “Mana 2” night by design: keep everything low‑stim and PG‑13. No graphic horror, no hospital/ICU imagery, no reality‑warping scenes that feel like the 2020 ICU stay. After the August 19, 2023 panic attack at Bonus Round Game Café running that high‑mana horror one‑shot — when I nearly passed out at the table and Dr. Marisa Feld at Northwestern Behavioral had to tweak my meds and tell me to keep game intensity under a “2 out of 3” — I promised her and Becca I’d respect that ceiling.

– Becca is joining after her Logan Square shift, and we’re still both twitchy about seizure triggers since her 03/03/2022 episode at the Logan Square apartment and the EEG at Rush. So: no strobing spell descriptions, no prolonged confinement/gaslighting scenes that echo her 2021 Northwestern harassment case.

– Lena is sleeping over in the office again (second weekend in a row) because of the February 2024 Cook County family‑court schedule. Her mom might swing by late. Hard rule: absolutely no child‑endangerment or cult‑targets‑kids content while she’s in the apartment — we already scared her once with the body‑horror playtest on 11/11/2023 and paid for it with three nights of nightmares and a tense Lurie visit.

– Open with a light social scene in Highmere, end by 10 sharp so I’m not wrecked for Sunday drive to Oak Park.

Hi Dr. Hart,

You asked last session how much of my “coping via worldbuilding” is conscious, so here’s a probably overlong answer while it’s fresh.

The Veiled Expanse (Plane 1) is very literally my Brown Line panic from March 2023 between Damen and Western stretched into a setting. In my June 10, 2024 Veiled Gate guest‑run at Bonus Round, I described it to the table as “a gray train car where you can’t move and the announcements never quite resolve.” My Fitbit says those sleep‑paralysis episodes last 3–4 minutes, but in my notes time inside the Expanse can feel like hours. That’s not an accident.

Same with the Ember Deep (Plane 2). I built the suffocating heat and dizzy low‑gravity pockets straight out of Dad’s second heart scare at Loyola in August 2021 — that sweltering cardiac step‑down room where Mom refused to touch the thermostat because he “needed to sweat it out,” and I almost fainted after 40 minutes standing by the bed arguing with the on‑call cardiologist about his nitro dose. When I write about “heat‑flux spikes” along the fire‑ley lines, I’m really just talking about my own burnout crash this past winter, juggling the Midwest Health Alliance LMS migration and three Veiled Gate contracts until I nearly blacked out on the Blue Line coming home from a corporate one‑shot in the Loop.

I know it sounds dramatic, but putting it into Avaris makes it feel…containable? Like I can walk my players through the foggy train car or the too‑hot ward and then close the book.

See you Wednesday at 5:30.

Jordan

metadata.subject: More on how the planes map to real life

artifacts.content:
Jordan: you still up? I’m staring at my campaign doc and realizing how much of Book 3 is just our lives with different nouns.

Sam: lol always up, what’s going on?

Jordan: The Veil‑sickness tables are literally my Oct–Dec 2020 burnout treatment with Dr. Priya Narang at Rush outpatient — 3 a.m. wake‑ups, panic in Zooms, bumping sertraline to 50 mg — just with “FP loss” slapped on top. And the corrupted healing subplot is Becca’s March–April 2022 postpartum stay on Northwestern’s 6E psych unit with the serial numbers barely filed off.

Sam: oof. does B know that?

Jordan: We talked about it a bit after I re‑read her 6E journal last week. I promised I’d rewrite the “color draining from the world” stuff so it’s not a one‑to‑one of what she wrote in those entries.

Jordan: And then there’s the Ember Deep cult stealing kids, which is starting to look way too much like the February 2024 emergency custody hearing over Lena at Cook County family court + the supervised exchange at the Logan Square station where she cried and clung to Becca. I actually wrote “child clings to aunt and won’t let go” in the draft before my brain went WAIT.

Sam: yeah that’s…a lot. maybe flag those arcs as “for home only, not publication” for now?

Jordan: Yeah. And maybe talk to Dr. Hart about how far is too far with the autobiographical mechanics.

Subject: House rules doc v3 – can you sanity‑check?

Hey Alex,

I finally rewrote my generic GURPS house rules after the last couple of years of “learning experiences,” and I’d love your eyes on them before I send them to the new Saturday group.

Big additions:

– Safety: Lines/veils + X‑card are now hard requirements at every table, no exceptions. This is partly fallout from that July 2023 online D&D 5E thing where “Mark from Behavioral Health” (one of the remote MHA clients) had a full panic attack on Zoom after a torture scene. I never want to be in a position again where something I framed as “dark fantasy” sends someone spiraling. So: no torture, no self‑harm, fade‑to‑black on detailed legal procedures and interrogations (this also goes back to the May 2019 Logan Square coworking game where a player told me they were on probation and didn’t want cop/court content).

– Health: Because of my NaNoWriMo 2021 RSI flare and the six weeks of PT at Athletico on Milwaukee, I’ve codified “rulings first, rules later” and simplified grappling. No more Technical Grappling RAW, no 5‑hour boss fights like that June 9, 2023 Games Plus session that ended with a steroid injection at Rush and two days of limited typing for MHA.

– Attendance: After I had to cancel that paid Veiled Gate campaign mid‑arc in October 2020 while I was seeing Dr. Patel at Presence Behavioral for COVID‑era anxiety, I added explicit downtime rules and banned punitive XP penalties for missed sessions. If I have to tap out for mental health, the game shouldn’t collapse.

Does this read as reasonable, or like I’m overcorrecting?

– J
